title: linaria arvensis subsp. parviflora
intro: Linaria arvensis subsp. parviflora, also known as the small-flowered toadflax, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The small-flowered toadflax is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of gastrointestinal problems. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
